The MegaRAC Development Kit for the AST2600 BMC features hardware, tutorials, and access to MegaRAC firmware to help developers and engineers gain experience in sensor porting, management technologies such as Serial Over LAN (SOL) and UART, and virtual media (vMedia). This kit allows customers to begin immediate development and training projects using the MegaRAC Community Edition BMC Firmware with the AST2600 BMC from ASPEED Technology.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ custom_padding_last_edited=”on|phone” _builder_version=”4.27.4″ _module_preset=”default”   • Fan
  • AMI Series 997 Sensor Board
  • Host system

  • Projects to gain development experience:

  • Ethernet, serial and/or display cable(s)

_module_preset=”default” text_font=”Aptos||||||||” text_text_color=”#000000″ text_line_height=”1.3em” width=”100%” width_tablet=”100%” width_phone=”100%” width_last_edited=”on|phone” module_alignment=”left” custom_margin=”10px||10px||true|false” global_colors_info=”{}”]

The AMI Series 997 Sensor Board is designed to mimic the sensors that are typically found on data center server platforms. It allows developers to get started immediately using MegaRAC Community Edition BMC firmware on the AST2600 BMC, for valuable hands-on experience in sensor porting, management and vMedia.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.27.4″ _module_preset=”default” custom_padding=”20px||||false|false” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_toggle title=”Voltage & Power Meter” open_toggle_text_color=”#000000″ open_toggle_background_color=”#FFFFFF” closed_toggle_text_color=”#000000″ closed_toggle_background_color=”#FFFFFF” icon_color=”#000000″ toggle_icon=”$||divi||400″ use_icon_font_size=”on” icon_font_size=”24px” open_icon_color=”#000000″ open_toggle_icon=”!||divi||400″ open_use_icon_font_size=”on” open_icon_font_size=”24px” disabled_on=”off|off|off” _builder_version=”4.27.4″ _module_preset=”default” title_text_color=”#000000″ title_font=”–et_global_heading_font|700||on|||||” title_font_size=”14px” title_letter_spacing=”3px” title_line_height=”2em” closed_title_font=”Aptos|700|||||||” body_font=”||||||||” body_line_height=”1.8em” custom_margin=”4px||20px|||” animation_style=”slide” animation_direction=”left” animation_delay=”200ms” animation_intensity_slide=”5%” border_radii=”on|10px|10px|10px|10px” global_colors_info=”{}”]

  • 2x Current/voltage sensors (INA219A) for 12V and 5V DC power monitoring
  • 2x Drive facing connectors for NVMe/SAS/SATA (SFF-8639 U.2 Slot; U.3 compatible)
  • 2x Host facing connectors for U.2/U.3 NVMe (x4 OCulink)
  • 2x Host facing connectors for SATA/SAS (standard 7-pin SATA)
  • 1x Backplane Controller (AMI MG9100) UBM/VPP/SGPIO host protocol for drive, LED and BMC management

[/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_toggle title=”Interface” open_toggle_text_color=”#000000″ open_toggle_background_color=”#FFFFFF” closed_toggle_text_color=”#000000″ closed_toggle_background_color=”#FFFFFF” icon_color=”#000000″ toggle_icon=”$||divi||400″ use_icon_font_size=”on” icon_font_size=”24px” open_icon_color=”#000000″ open_toggle_icon=”!||divi||400″ open_use_icon_font_size=”on” open_icon_font_size=”24px” disabled_on=”off|off|off” _builder_version=”4.27.4″ _module_preset=”default” title_text_color=”#000000″ title_font=”–et_global_heading_font|700||on|||||” title_font_size=”14px” title_letter_spacing=”3px” title_line_height=”2em” closed_title_font=”Aptos|700|||||||” body_font=”||||||||” body_line_height=”1.8em” custom_margin=”4px||20px|||” animation_style=”slide” animation_direction=”left” animation_delay=”200ms” animation_intensity_slide=”5%” border_radii=”on|10px|10px|10px|10px” global_colors_info=”{}”]

  • 1x NVMe I²C management interface (accessible via pin header)
  • 1x 2-Channel I²C MUX with Interrupt (PCA9543A)
  • 2x Temperature sensors (TM75AID)
  • 1x 16K Bit EEPROM (24LC16B)
  • 1x 2-to-1 I²C master de-multiplexer with an arbiter function (PCA9641)
  • 1x I²C repeater (PCA9617)
  • 1x Parallel-in/serial-out (74LV165A) with 8 position DIP switch

[/et_pb_toggle][et_pb_toggle title=”LED Display” open_toggle_text_color=”#000000″ open_toggle_background_color=”#FFFFFF” closed_toggle_text_color=”#000000″ closed_toggle_background_color=”#FFFFFF” icon_color=”#000000″ toggle_icon=”$||divi||400″ use_icon_font_size=”on” icon_font_size=”24px” open_icon_color=”#000000″ open_toggle_icon=”!||divi||400″ open_use_icon_font_size=”on” open_icon_font_size=”24px” disabled_on=”off|off|off” _builder_version=”4.27.4″ _module_preset=”default” title_text_color=”#000000″ title_font=”–et_global_heading_font|700||on|||||” title_font_size=”14px” title_letter_spacing=”3px” title_line_height=”2em” closed_title_font=”Aptos|700|||||||” body_font=”||||||||” body_line_height=”1.8em” custom_margin=”4px||20px|||” animation_style=”slide” animation_direction=”left” animation_delay=”200ms” animation_intensity_slide=”5%” border_radii=”on|10px|10px|10px|10px” global_colors_info=”{}”]

  • 2x Serial-in/parallel-out (74LV595A) with 7 segment LED display
  • 2x Potentiometers for +12V, +5V voltage monitoring (to BMC ADC input)
  • 1x 2-channel +12V DC FAN control (PWM)/monitoring (TACHO)
  • Signals are available via pin headers for composing desired I²C topologies
  • 4 GPIO controlled LEDs
  • 4 Digital input implementation with 4pcs tactile switches

[/et_pb_toggle][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row use_custom_gutter=”on” gutter_width=”1″ make_equal=”on” custom_padding_last_edited=”on|phone” _builder_version=”4.27.4″ _module_preset=”default” width=”90%” max_width=”2560px” custom_padding=”20px||20px||false|false” custom_padding_tablet=”20px||30px||false|false” custom_padding_phone=”0px||10px||false|false” custom_css_main_element=”align-items:flex-end;” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.24.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_heading title=”For More Information” _builder_version=”4.27.4″ _module_preset=”default” title_level=”h2″ title_font=”Aptos||||||||” title_text_align=”center” title_text_color=”#000000″ title_font_size=”45px” width=”80%” width_tablet=”90%” width_phone=”100%” width_last_edited=”on|phone” module_alignment=”center” custom_margin=”10px||0px||false|false” custom_padding=”||||false|false” title_font_size_tablet=”40px” title_font_size_phone=”32px” title_font_size_last_edited=”on|phone” global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_heading][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.4″ _module_preset=”default” text_font=”Aptos||||||||” text_text_color=”#000000″ text_line_height=”1.3em” text_orientation=”center” width=”70%” width_tablet=”90%” width_phone=”100%” width_last_edited=”on|phone” module_alignment=”center” custom_margin=”0px||10px||false|false” global_colors_info=”{}”]

For BMC firmware training and development with MegaRAC Community Edition

Deliver faster. Deliver experience.Deliver confidence.

The MegaRAC Development Kit for the […]
